title: For Annie by Edgar Allan Poe — Love Poetry Lines
content:
Thank Heaven! the crisis--
  The danger is past,
And the lingering illness
  Is over at last--
And the fever called "Living"
  Is conquered at last.

Sadly, I know,
meaning: "For Annie" by Edgar Allan Poe is a love and deep and nature and inspirational poem consisting of 116 lines. The poet explores themes of love and deep and nature and inspirational through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "Thank Heaven! the crisis--   The danger is past,...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Edgar Allan Poe's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
